title: Server Hardware & Infrastructure Questions
description: Server Hardware, BIOS, Networking, RAID, Storage, and Data Center Interview Questions
questions: What is BIOS and what are its main functions in a server?
What happens during POST (Power-On Self-Test)?
What is the purpose of PCIe slots in a server?
What is a hot-swappable component?
What is a MAC address?
What is the purpose of a MAC address?
What is an IP address?
What is the difference between an IP address and a MAC address?
What is out-of-band management and why is it important?
What are iDRAC and iLO?
What are the key steps to configure iDRAC?
How would you access a server if the operating system is down?
Why do servers have dual power supplies?
Do you have experience with rack and stack of servers?
What are the steps involved in the rack and stack process?
What is a KVM switch and what is its purpose?
You have 8 physical disks of different sizes—how can you combine them into one logical storage unit?
What is RAID and why is it used in servers?
What are the advantages of combining multiple disks into one logical volume?
What are the disadvantages of combining multiple disks into one logical unit?
Explain RAID 0, RAID 1, RAID 5, RAID 6, and RAID 10.
Which RAID level provides the best performance and which provides the best redundancy?
Which RAID level would you choose for critical database servers and why?
What happens when a disk fails in RAID 5 or RAID 6?
What is a RAID controller card and why is it used?
What is the difference between hardware RAID and software RAID?
How does RAID affect performance and capacity?
How does RAID improve fault tolerance?
